summary: A CLI code generator that transforms Conjure API IR (Intermediate Representation) definitions into strongly-typed TypeScript interfaces and HTTP client classes. The tool reads a JSON IR file produced by the Conjure compiler and emits TypeScript source files for four categories: object types (interfaces), enum types (namespace + type alias pattern), union types (discriminated unions with visitor pattern, type guards, and factory functions), and service definitions (interface + class with IHttpApiBridge-based RPC methods). Uses ts-morph (TypeScript AST manipulation library) for programmatic code generation rather than string templates. Supports both throwing and non-throwing (Result-based) service variants, flavorized/branded type aliases, readonly interfaces, and structured error types with type guard functions. Conforms to Conjure RFC 002 for generator contracts and outputs npm-publishable packages with package.json and tsconfig.json.

ontologyConcepts.evidence: Conjure object definitions are generated as TypeScript interfaces prefixed with 'I'. Each field in the Conjure definition maps to a property with its resolved TypeScript type. The `generateObject()` function in generateType.ts processes `IObjectDefinition` from conjure-api, resolving field types through `resolveTsType()` and generating import declarations for cross-package references. Optional fields get `?` tokens. Example output: `export interface IManyFieldExample { 'string': string; 'integer': number; 'optionalItem'?: string | null; 'items': Array<string>; }`.

Conjure field definitions are translated to TypeScript interface properties via `resolveTsType()`. The type mapping is: STRING/DATETIME/RID/BEARERTOKEN/UUID -> string, INTEGER/SAFELONG -> number, DOUBLE -> number | 'NaN', BOOLEAN -> boolean, BINARY -> ReadableStream<Uint8Array> (top-level) or string (nested), ANY -> any, optional<T> -> T | null, list<T>/set<T> -> Array<T> (or ReadonlyArray<T>), map<K,V> -> { [key: string]: V } (or { [key in EnumType]?: V } for enum keys). Property names are single-quoted in the generated interface to handle reserved keywords and dashed names.

Not directly present. conjure-typescript is a code generator that produces HTTP client stubs. Authentication is handled at the runtime layer via conjure-client's IHttpApiBridge, which accepts a Bearer token header parameter. The generator maps BEARERTOKEN Conjure primitive type to TypeScript 'string' and can handle header-type parameters, but does not generate any access control logic itself.

Not directly present. Conjure's type system does not have a dedicated 'link type' concept. Inter-object references are expressed as reference types (one object type field having the type of another object), which the generator resolves via `resolveTsTypeForReferenceType()` and generates appropriate import declarations via `resolveImportsForReferenceType()`.

Conjure service endpoints serve as the equivalent of 'actions' in the Ontology sense. Each endpoint in an IServiceDefinition becomes a method on the generated service class. The generator handles HTTP method, path, body/header/query parameters, path parameter extraction from URL templates, request/response media type negotiation, and return type resolution. Endpoints can have arguments categorized as body, header, query, or path parameters.
Not present. conjure-typescript generates RPC-style service clients, not ontology function definitions.

The generator reads Conjure IR (Intermediate Representation) as its input, which is a JSON format produced by the Conjure compiler from .conjure.yml definition files. The IR contains a complete API specification with types, services, and errors. The command loads this via `loadConjureDefinition()` which reads and parses the JSON file, defaulting empty arrays for errors, services, and types. The generator produces npm-publishable packages with generated package.json (including conjure-client dependency) and tsconfig.json.
Not present. This is a pure code generation tool with no AI/LLM components.

Every Conjure object type, union type, error type, and service generates a TypeScript interface prefixed with 'I'. Objects produce `IObjectName` interfaces. Unions produce per-variant interfaces (`IUnionName_VariantName`) plus a union type alias. Services produce `IServiceName` interface (method signatures) plus a concrete `ServiceName` class implementing the interface. Errors produce `IErrorName` interfaces with errorCode, errorInstanceId, errorName, and parameters fields.

apiSurface.name: conjure-typescript generate
apiSurface.description: The primary CLI command that reads a Conjure IR JSON file and generates TypeScript source code. Takes positional args for input (IR file path) and output (directory). Supports flags: --packageName, --packageVersion, --flavorizedAliases, --nodeCompatibleModules, --rawSource, --readonlyInterfaces, --productDependencies, --generateThrowingServices (default true), --generateNonThrowingServices (default false). Conforms to Conjure generator RFC 002.

apiSurface.name: IConjureDefinition
apiSurface.description: The root input type representing a complete Conjure API specification. Contains three arrays: `types` (ITypeDefinition[] - alias, enum, object, union), `services` (IServiceDefinition[] - endpoints with args, returns, HTTP metadata), and `errors` (IErrorDefinition[] - structured error types). This is the JSON IR format produced by the Conjure compiler.

apiSurface.name: ITypeDefinition
apiSurface.description: Represents a Conjure type definition, discriminated by a `type` field with variants: 'alias' (IAliasDefinition), 'enum' (IEnumDefinition), 'object' (IObjectDefinition), 'union' (IUnionDefinition). Each variant contains a `typeName` (ITypeName with package and name) and variant-specific fields. Type guards like `ITypeDefinition.isAlias()` are provided.

apiSurface.name: IType
apiSurface.description: Represents a Conjure type reference within field definitions. Variants: 'primitive' (PrimitiveType enum), 'list' (IListType with itemType), 'set' (ISetType with itemType), 'map' (IMapType with keyType/valueType), 'optional' (IOptionalType with itemType), 'reference' (ITypeName pointing to a named type), 'external' (IExternalReference with fallback). Type guards like `IType.isPrimitive()` are provided.

apiSurface.name: PrimitiveType
apiSurface.description: Enumeration of Conjure primitive types: STRING, DATETIME, INTEGER, DOUBLE, SAFELONG, BINARY, ANY, BOOLEAN, UUID, RID, BEARERTOKEN. Each maps to specific TypeScript types via resolveTsTypeForPrimitiveType().

apiSurface.name: IServiceDefinition
apiSurface.description: Represents a Conjure service with a serviceName (ITypeName), optional docs, and an array of IEndpointDefinition. Each endpoint has endpointName, httpMethod, httpPath, args (IArgumentDefinition[] with paramType: body/header/query/path), returns (optional IType), docs, deprecated flag, tags, and errors (IEndpointError[]).

apiSurface.name: IHttpApiBridge
apiSurface.description: The runtime HTTP bridge interface that generated service classes depend on. Generated service constructors accept an IHttpApiBridge instance. Service methods call `this.bridge.call<T>(serviceName, endpointName, httpMethod, httpPath, data, headers, queryParams, pathParams, requestMediaType, responseMediaType)` to make RPC calls. Provided by conjure-client/conjure-typescript-runtime.

apiSurface.name: IConjureResult<T, E>
apiSurface.description: Result type used by non-throwing service variants. Non-throwing endpoint methods return `Promise<IConjureResult<ResultType, ErrorType>>` where success is `{ status: 'success', result: T }` and failure is `{ status: 'failure', error: E }`. The error type is a union of the endpoint's declared error interfaces.

apiSurface.name: generate()
apiSurface.description: The core generator function in generator.ts. Takes an IConjureDefinition, output directory, ITypeGenerationFlags, and IServiceGenerationFlags. Computes known types map, validates service names, creates output directories, then concurrently generates type files, service files, error files, and index files using SimpleAst.

apiSurface.name: SimpleAst
apiSurface.description: Wrapper around ts-morph's Project class that manages source file creation and index file generation. Creates TypeScript source files at computed paths based on ITypeName (package segments become directory names). Generates barrel index.ts files for each module directory and a root index.ts with either re-exports (single module) or namespace imports (multiple modules).

apiSurface.name: resolveTsType()
apiSurface.description: The central type resolution function that converts a Conjure IType into a TypeScript type string. Handles all seven IType variants recursively. Key mappings: primitives to TS primitives, list/set to Array<T>/ReadonlyArray<T>, map to object type with string/enum keys, optional to T | null, reference to I-prefixed type name (with alias inlining for non-flavorized aliases, and IUnion.IUnion for unions), external to fallback type.

apiSurface.name: resolveImports()
apiSurface.description: Recursively resolves import declarations needed for a given Conjure IType. Handles cross-package references by computing relative paths between type directories. Produces named imports for most types and namespace imports for union types (since unions use the pattern IUnion.IUnion). Deduplicates and sorts imports via sortImports().

architecturePatterns.description: Rather than using string templates, the generator uses ts-morph (a TypeScript AST manipulation library built on top of the TypeScript compiler API) to programmatically construct source files. Each generator function creates a SourceFile, adds imports/interfaces/classes/functions/variables as AST structures, calls formatText() for consistent formatting, and save() to write to disk. This provides correct TypeScript formatting, import management, and structural validation at generation time.

The conjure-api package provides a fully discriminated union type system modeled on Conjure's type algebra. ITypeDefinition discriminates on 'alias'|'enum'|'object'|'union', IType discriminates on 'primitive'|'list'|'set'|'map'|'optional'|'reference'|'external', and IParameterType discriminates on 'body'|'header'|'query'|'path'. Each variant provides static type guards (e.g., ITypeDefinition.isAlias()) and factory functions (e.g., ITypeDefinition.object()). The entire generator pipeline dispatches on these discriminants.

Before generation, all type definitions are indexed into a Map<string, ITypeDefinition> keyed by a hashable type name (package + separator + name). This registry enables O(1) type lookups during resolution, allowing resolveTsType() and resolveImports() to dereference type references, chase alias chains, determine if a reference is to an enum (no 'I' prefix) vs. union (needs namespace import) vs. object (named import), and compute relative import paths.

Conjure union types generate a rich TypeScript pattern: per-variant interfaces (IUnion_Variant with value + type discriminant), factory functions (to construct variant values), type guard functions (isVariant with type narrowing), a Visitor interface (with a handler per variant + unknown), and a visit() function. All are bundled into a const object exported under the union name, enabling usage like `IMyUnion.isVariant(x)`, `IMyUnion.variant(value)`, and `IMyUnion.visit(x, visitor)`. Reserved keyword handling appends underscore to factory function names.

Conjure enums are generated as TypeScript namespaces containing both type aliases and const values for each enum member, plus a top-level type alias as `keyof typeof EnumName`. This avoids TypeScript's built-in enum (which has cross-library compatibility issues) while providing both value-level and type-level access. Empty enums special-case to an exported const {} with a void type alias.

Each Conjure service generates two exports: an interface (IServiceName with method signatures returning Promise<T>) and a concrete class (ServiceName) that implements the interface. The class takes an IHttpApiBridge in its constructor and each method delegates to `this.bridge.call<T>()` with the full endpoint metadata (service name, endpoint name, HTTP method, path, body, headers, query params, path params, request/response media types). This enables dependency injection of the HTTP transport.

Services can be generated in two flavors: throwing (methods throw on API errors, return Promise<T>) and non-throwing (methods catch errors and return Promise<IConjureResult<T, ErrorUnion>>). Non-throwing services are suffixed with 'WithErrors' and their bridge calls chain `.then(result => ({ status: 'success', result })).catch(error => ({ status: 'failure', error }))`. Streaming (binary) endpoints in non-throwing services still throw, as streams cannot be safely wrapped in Result types.

When --flavorizedAliases is enabled, compatible aliases (string, number, rid, uuid, safelong, bearertoken - but NOT any, boolean, binary, datetime) generate branded type aliases using intersection types: `export type IMyAlias = string & { __conjure_type?: 'MyAlias'; __conjure_package?: 'com.palantir.product'; }`. This provides nominal typing (flavor) in TypeScript's structural type system, preventing accidental mixing of semantically different string types.

Conjure package names (e.g., 'com.palantir.product') are mapped to directory names by dropping the first two segments and joining the rest with hyphens (e.g., 'product'). Type names are lowercased for file names. Cross-package imports compute relative paths between directories. Multi-module outputs get a root index.ts with namespace imports for each module directory.

architecturePatterns.pattern: Structured Error Types with Type Guards
architecturePatterns.description: Conjure error definitions generate TypeScript interfaces with four fields: errorCode (literal string type like 'INVALID_ARGUMENT'), errorInstanceId (string), errorName (literal string 'Namespace:ErrorName'), and parameters (object type with typed fields from safe/unsafe args). A companion type guard function `isErrorName(arg: any): arg is IErrorName` is generated, checking the errorName discriminant. Non-throwing services include these error types in their IConjureResult error union.

typeDefinitions.name: IConjureDefinition
typeDefinitions.definition: interface IConjureDefinition { types: ITypeDefinition[]; services: IServiceDefinition[]; errors: IErrorDefinition[]; }
typeDefinitions.purpose: Root IR type representing a complete Conjure API specification. This is the JSON structure read from disk as input to the generator. The `types` array contains all type definitions (aliases, enums, objects, unions), `services` contains all service definitions with their endpoints, and `errors` contains all structured error definitions.

typeDefinitions.name: ITypeDefinition
typeDefinitions.definition: type ITypeDefinition = { type: 'alias'; alias: IAliasDefinition } | { type: 'enum'; enum: IEnumDefinition } | { type: 'object'; object: IObjectDefinition } | { type: 'union'; union: IUnionDefinition }
typeDefinitions.purpose: Discriminated union representing the four kinds of Conjure type definitions. Each variant wraps a definition-specific interface containing the typeName (ITypeName) and variant-specific fields (e.g., fields for objects, values for enums, union members for unions, alias target for aliases).

typeDefinitions.name: IType
typeDefinitions.definition: type IType = { type: 'primitive'; primitive: PrimitiveType } | { type: 'list'; list: IListType } | { type: 'set'; set: ISetType } | { type: 'map'; map: IMapType } | { type: 'optional'; optional: IOptionalType } | { type: 'reference'; reference: ITypeName } | { type: 'external'; external: IExternalReference }
typeDefinitions.purpose: Discriminated union representing a Conjure type reference used within field definitions, endpoint parameters, and return types. Primitive types map directly to TS types, container types (list, set, map, optional) wrap inner IType references recursively, reference points to a named type definition, and external provides a fallback type for cross-language external types.

typeDefinitions.name: ITypeName
typeDefinitions.definition: interface ITypeName { package: string; name: string; }
typeDefinitions.purpose: Fully qualified name for a Conjure type or service. The package is a dot-separated namespace (e.g., 'com.palantir.product') and name is the type/service name (e.g., 'ManyFieldExample'). Used for directory/file path computation, import resolution, and type registry keying.

typeDefinitions.name: PrimitiveType
typeDefinitions.definition: enum PrimitiveType { STRING, DATETIME, INTEGER, DOUBLE, SAFELONG, BINARY, ANY, BOOLEAN, UUID, RID, BEARERTOKEN }
typeDefinitions.purpose: Enumeration of all Conjure primitive types. Each maps to specific TypeScript output: STRING/DATETIME/RID/BEARERTOKEN/UUID -> 'string', INTEGER/SAFELONG -> 'number', DOUBLE -> 'number | "NaN"', BOOLEAN -> 'boolean', BINARY -> 'ReadableStream<Uint8Array>' (top-level) or 'string' (nested), ANY -> 'any'.

typeDefinitions.name: ITypeGenerationFlags
typeDefinitions.definition: interface ITypeGenerationFlags { readonly flavorizedAliases: boolean; readonly readonlyInterfaces: boolean; }
typeDefinitions.purpose: Configuration flags controlling type generation behavior. flavorizedAliases enables branded/flavored type aliases using intersection types with __conjure_type/__conjure_package phantom fields. readonlyInterfaces makes all interface properties readonly and uses ReadonlyArray instead of Array for list/set types.

typeDefinitions.name: IServiceGenerationFlags
typeDefinitions.definition: interface IServiceGenerationFlags { readonly generateThrowingServices: boolean; readonly generateNonThrowingServices: boolean; }
typeDefinitions.purpose: Configuration flags controlling service generation. generateThrowingServices (default true) produces standard service classes whose methods throw on API errors. generateNonThrowingServices (default false) produces 'WithErrors' suffixed service variants whose methods return IConjureResult<T, E> instead of throwing.

typeDefinitions.name: IEndpointDefinition
typeDefinitions.definition: interface IEndpointDefinition { endpointName: string; httpMethod: string; httpPath: string; args: IArgumentDefinition[]; returns?: IType; docs?: string; deprecated?: string; tags?: string[]; errors?: IEndpointError[]; }
typeDefinitions.purpose: Represents a single RPC endpoint within a service. Contains the HTTP metadata (method, path with {param} placeholders), typed arguments (each with a paramType discriminant for body/header/query/path), optional return type, documentation, deprecation notice, tags (like 'incubating'), and declared error types.

typeDefinitions.name: IPackageJson
typeDefinitions.definition: interface IPackageJson { name: string; version: string; main?: string; types?: string; dependencies: Record<string, string>; devDependencies: Record<string, string>; scripts: Record<string, string>; sls?: { dependencies?: Record<string, IProductDependency> }; sideEffects?: boolean; /* ... */ }
typeDefinitions.purpose: TypeScript representation of the generated package.json file. The generator produces npm packages with conjure-client as a runtime dependency, typescript as a dev dependency, 'tsc' as the build script, and optional SLS (Service Lifecycle System) product dependency metadata for Palantir's internal deployment infrastructure.

typeDefinitions.name: IErrorDefinition
typeDefinitions.definition: interface IErrorDefinition { errorName: ITypeName; namespace: string; code: string; docs?: string; safeArgs: IFieldDefinition[]; unsafeArgs: IFieldDefinition[]; }
typeDefinitions.purpose: Represents a Conjure error definition. The errorName is the fully qualified type name, namespace is used in the generated errorName discriminant (e.g., 'Metadata:PrimitiveError'), code is the HTTP error category (e.g., 'INVALID_ARGUMENT'), and safeArgs/unsafeArgs define the error's typed parameters. Safe args are safe to log, unsafe args may contain sensitive data.

typeDefinitions.name: Generated Union Type Pattern
typeDefinitions.definition: export interface IUnion_Variant { 'variantField': FieldType; 'type': 'variantName'; } export type IUnion = IUnion_Variant1 | IUnion_Variant2; export interface IUnionVisitor<T> { 'variant1': (obj: Type1) => T; 'unknown': (obj: IUnion) => T; } export const IUnion = { isVariant: fn, variant: factory, visit: fn }
typeDefinitions.purpose: The generated pattern for Conjure union types. Each variant becomes a separate interface with the variant value and a 'type' string literal discriminant. A type alias unions all variants. A Visitor interface maps variant names to handler functions with an 'unknown' catch-all. Factory functions create variant instances, type guards narrow the union, and visit() dispatches through the visitor. All are bundled as properties on a const object for namespace-like access.

dependencies.name: conjure-api
dependencies.purpose: Provides TypeScript types for the Conjure IR (Intermediate Representation) format: IConjureDefinition, ITypeDefinition, IType, ITypeName, PrimitiveType, IServiceDefinition, IEndpointDefinition, IErrorDefinition, and all their sub-types. Also provides type guards (e.g., ITypeDefinition.isAlias()) and factory functions (e.g., ITypeDefinition.object()). This is the core data model that the generator processes.

dependencies.name: conjure-client
dependencies.purpose: Provides the IHttpApiBridge interface type used in generated service constructors, the MediaType enum used to resolve request/response content types, and IConjureResult type for non-throwing service variants. Also becomes a dependency in generated packages' package.json so the generated code can import IHttpApiBridge at runtime.

dependencies.name: ts-morph
dependencies.purpose: TypeScript AST manipulation library used for all code generation. Provides Project, SourceFile, and various structure types (InterfaceDeclarationStructure, FunctionDeclarationStructure, ClassDeclarationStructure, ImportDeclarationStructure, etc.) for programmatically building TypeScript source files. Also used for code formatting and file writing.

dependencies.name: yargs
dependencies.purpose: CLI argument parsing framework. The GenerateCommand class implements yargs' CommandModule interface, defining positional args (input, output), options (packageName, packageVersion, flags), and the handler function. The main entry point registers the command and calls yargs.parse().

dependencies.name: fs-extra
dependencies.purpose: Extended filesystem operations used throughout the generator: reading IR JSON files, creating output directories (mkdirp), removing directories on error (removeSync), checking directory existence (existsSync), and writing generated files.

dependencies.name: lodash
dependencies.purpose: Used for utility functions, primarily `_.uniqBy()` in the generator to deduplicate known type definitions by package name when creating output directories.

dependencies.name: sls-version
dependencies.purpose: Palantir's SLS (Service Lifecycle System) version validation library. Used to validate that --packageVersion conforms to SLS version format and to parse product dependency version constraints.

dependencies.name: semver
dependencies.purpose: Semantic versioning utilities, likely used for version comparison and validation alongside sls-version.

dependencies.name: typescript
dependencies.purpose: TypeScript compiler for building the generator itself. Also referenced in generated package.json devDependencies so generated code can be compiled.

dependencies.name: jest
dependencies.purpose: Test framework for unit tests. Tests compare generated TypeScript output against snapshot/fixture files in the resources directory.

dependencies.name: webpack
dependencies.purpose: Bundles the generator into a single distributable file in dist/ for the CLI binary. The bin/conjure-typescript script invokes the webpack-bundled output.
